overview.shortDescription: A social apex predator of African savannas and grasslands. Lions live in prides, hunt cooperatively, and help regulate herbivore populations.
overview.whyItMatters: Apex predators shape food webs and ecosystem balance; their presence can indicate healthy savanna systems.

identificationTips: Look for tawny coat, tufted tail tip, and adult male mane
Prides often rest in shade during midday
identity.identificationConfidence: High for adults at close ethical range

habitatRange.inGameClue: Explore the Savanna region at dawn or dusk

bodyTraits.sexualDimorphism: Adult males often have a prominent mane

ecology.ecosystemImpact: Helps maintain balance in large herbivore communities

conservation.whyConservationMatters: Losing apex predators can trigger herbivore overpopulation and ecosystem imbalance

earthSystems.climateSensitivity: Affected by prey availability, water, fire regimes, and drought stress

humanConnections.humanWildlifeConflict: Can conflict with livestock owners near protected areas

unknownToScience: Full pride dynamics across all populations

overview.shortDescription: Earth's largest land mammal — a keystone herbivore that engineers savanna and forest landscapes.
overview.whyItMatters: Elephants create water access, disperse seeds, and maintain open habitats used by many species.

dietFoodWeb.foodWebDiagram: Plants → Elephant → Nutrient cycling + habitat modification

ecology.ecosystemImpact: Creates trails, water holes, and tree gaps that benefit biodiversity

overview.shortDescription: A long-distance migrating butterfly whose orange wings warn predators of toxicity acquired from milkweed host plants.
overview.whyItMatters: Pollinator and indicator of plant community health; famous for multi-generational migration.

fieldMarks: Orange wings with black veins and white spots

lifeCycle.metamorphosis: Complete metamorphosis: egg → larva → pupa → adult

conservation.whyConservationMatters: Declining milkweed and migration routes threaten North American populations

earthSystems.climateSensitivity: Migration timing linked to temperature and nectar resource seasons

overview.shortDescription: The largest animal known to have ever lived — a filter-feeding baleen whale of the open ocean.
overview.whyItMatters: Whales move nutrients through ocean food webs and store carbon; their recovery shows conservation can work.

habitatRange.migration: Seasonal migration between feeding and breeding areas

earthSystems.climateSensitivity: Depends on ocean productivity, temperature, and krill bloom timing

overview.shortDescription: An extinct class of marine arthropods that dominated Paleozoic seas for nearly 300 million years.
overview.whyItMatters: Trilobites are index fossils — they help scientists date rock layers and understand early animal evolution.

identity.debatedClassification: Exact relationships among trilobite orders remain under study

massExtinctionEvents: End-Permian extinction ended last trilobite groups

conservation.whyConservationMatters: Ancient extinction teaches us how vulnerable even dominant groups can be

behavior.speculationWarning: Most behavior inferred from fossils and modern arthropod analogs

uncertainty.fossilUncertainty: Soft anatomy rarely preserved; many species known from exoskeletons only

overview.shortDescription: A top Cambrian predator with grasping appendages and a circular mouth — icon of the Cambrian explosion.
overview.whyItMatters: Shows early complex predator-prey dynamics in marine ecosystems.

identity.debatedClassification: Once reconstructed from parts thought to be separate species

uncertainty.fossilUncertainty: Reconstruction history includes famous misassembly of fossil parts

overview.shortDescription: An Ediacaran organism with a quilted oval body — among the first large complex multicellular life forms.
overview.whyItMatters: Helps scientists study life before the Cambrian explosion and the origins of animals.

identity.debatedClassification: Affinity uncertain — may be animal, lichen-like, or other; active scientific debate

behavior.speculationWarning: Almost all biology inferred; no modern exact analog

overview.shortDescription: Photosynthetic bacteria that produce oxygen and build stromatolites — among Earth's oldest life forms.
overview.whyItMatters: Cyanobacteria helped oxygenate Earth's atmosphere during the Great Oxidation Event.

conservation.whyConservationMatters: Modern blooms can affect water quality; ancient forms changed the planet permanently

uncertainty.notes: Represents a phylum-level group, not a single species.

overview.shortDescription: A classic Cambrian trilobite species known from well-preserved Utah fossils.
overview.whyItMatters: Common in educational fossil collections; helps date Cambrian marine rocks.

overview.shortDescription: A gigantic Late Jurassic sauropod with forelimbs longer than hindlimbs — among the largest land animals ever.
overview.whyItMatters: Shows the extreme body-size evolution of dinosaurs and Morrison Formation ecosystems.
